summary: Strong dialectical reasoning throughout: both sides ground arguments in quantified material conditions, identify genuine structural contradictions (including self-identified tensions), and trace explicit dependency chains with named broken links. Deductions: Skeptic R1 dependencies assert negative learning curve without full structural tracing; both sides' R2 resolutions propose policy mechanisms underspecified in implementation; R3 both sides partially restate prior evidence rather than introducing new material conditions; R3 Skeptic contradiction ('alongside not instead') is a logical inconsistency rather than a deep structural tension. Verdict excels by sequencing six condition-changes in dependency order and honestly naming four unresolved dependencies.
